response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong architectural thinking, distributed systems experience, and proven mentoring capability through workshops and code dojos, they fall short on two critical minimum requirements: only ~6 months of C++ experience versus the required 8 years, and no explicit technical leadership role in their title history (all positions are Developer/Engineer/Founder rather than Technical Lead or Engineering Manager). The resume shows a strong full-stack engineer who could grow into this role, but lacks the foundational C++ depth and demonstrated technical leadership needed for immediate impact in this mid-senior platform leadership position.
strengths: Proven distributed systems and large-scale infrastructure expertise from Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second) and recent cloud work with AWS CDK and Kubernetes

Exceptional software craftsmanship and testing culture—authored testing frameworks, created TDD courses, organized code dojos, and demonstrated commitment to engineering excellence across multiple companies
Demonstrated teaching and mentoring ability through workshops, courses, and open-source projects that have reached production use (GISCollective with Green Map, trial test runner)
concerns: Critical gap in C++ experience: only ~6 months at National Instruments versus the required 8 years; career focus has been primarily web, Node.js, and D language rather than systems-level C++

No explicit technical leadership experience in title or responsibility—resume shows senior developer/engineer/founder roles but not Technical Lead, Engineering Manager, or formal mentorship of engineering teams
Cloud platform architecture experience is secondary to product engineering; cloud work (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) is recent and incidental to product work rather than core infrastructure platform focus

key_factors.factor: Lack of explicit technical leadership role
Strong distributed systems and architecture foundation
